Say nothing.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Confrontation</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He stops in the doorway. Sees the ledger open. His face goes white.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Then angry. Not at her — at the situation. At being caught. At the look on her face that says she already did the math.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> That&#x27;s not your concern.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> Forty thousand dollars isn&#x27;t my concern?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I said I&#x27;ll handle it.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His jaw is tight. The controlled man, controlled. But cracking.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> You&#x27;re charging me fifty a week for food money you can&#x27;t afford to lose. You offered me a job doing books that prove you&#x27;re broke. And Diana doesn&#x27;t know.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Silence. The clock ticks. He sits down. Not behind the desk — in the other chair. Her chair. Like the power just shifted and even the furniture knows it.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> No. Diana doesn&#x27;t know.</blockquote></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Protection</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She closes the drawer. Slides the ledger back under. Smooths the papers on top.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>By the time he walks in, she&#x27;s working on receivables. The correct ledger. The safe numbers.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Everything in order?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> Everything&#x27;s fine.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He looks at her. A beat too long. Something in his eyes — does he know she found it? Does he suspect?</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I carry his secret now. He doesn&#x27;t even know I have it.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Forty thousand dollars of silence between us. A bond he didn&#x27;t ask for and I didn&#x27;t plan.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_power_outage" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Candlelight</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_power_outage</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Dark</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Thunder. The lights flicker twice, then die. The whole property goes black.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The fridge hum stops. The house is suddenly, completely silent except for rain on the roof.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_power_outage_dark.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank finds candles. Six of them, arranged on the coffee table. The living room flickers amber. For the first time, all four of them are in the same room without electronics as a buffer.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank opens whiskey. Even @jake gets a glass.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Tell me a story.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Stories</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank talks about building the house. How the roof leaked for two years. How @ryan fell through the bathroom floor when he was twelve.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I didn&#x27;t fall through. The floor gave out.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Same thing.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>They&#x27;re laughing. All of them. The sound is strange — she&#x27;s heard them talk, argue, eat in silence. She hasn&#x27;t heard them laugh together.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@jake sketches by candlelight. Quick portraits. @ryan, leaning back, one arm over the couch. @frank, whiskey in hand, face half in shadow. Her, watching all of them.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Then @ryan tells a real story.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Eighteen. Job site. A board slipped off the saw table and I caught it with my arm instead of letting it fall.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He pushes up his sleeve. The scar. Candlelight catches the raised line.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I thought it was broken. I didn&#x27;t want to cry in front of the crew.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Silence. @frank looks at his whiskey.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I drove you to the ER. Didn&#x27;t say a word the whole way.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> You were fine.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I know. That&#x27;s why you didn&#x27;t talk.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The storm fades. The candles get lower. Nobody moves to check the breaker.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She looks at them — @frank with his whiskey, @ryan sprawled on the couch, @jake drawing by dying light. For one moment she sees them clearly: just three men in a house that&#x27;s too quiet, each carrying something they don&#x27;t talk about.</p></div> <div class="review-block block-other"></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;I&#x27;m glad I&#x27;m here.&quot;</li><li>Lean against @ryan.</li><li>Lean against @jake.</li><li>&quot;Thank you for the stories.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_franks_late_night" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Three Inches</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_franks_late_night</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Table</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Kitchen. Past midnight. The house is asleep.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank is at the table. Undershirt. Barefoot. The whiskey is half gone — not tonight, over time. The bottle lives in the cabinet above the stove.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He poured her glass before she came down. It&#x27;s sitting at the chair across from him. Still full.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He knew I&#x27;d come down. The glass was already there.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She sits. Drinks. The whiskey burns. The kitchen is dark except for the lamp over the stove — everything in amber.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_franks_late_night_two_am.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He talks. About Diana. About the business. About the house — how he built the addition himself, the summer @ryan was thirteen and held boards he couldn&#x27;t lift.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His hand is on the table. Three inches from hers.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Neither of them moves it closer. Neither of them moves it away.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> You should go to bed.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He means it. He also doesn&#x27;t mean it. She can hear both.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Touch his hand. Just her pinkie on his.</li><li>&quot;Good night, Frank.&quot; Leave the whiskey unfinished.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_jake_sketches" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Hidden Drawings</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_jake_sketches</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Under the Mattress</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@jake is in the shower. She&#x27;s in his room — waiting for him, looking at his portfolio. The usual drawings: the creek, the workshop, her hands.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>A page sticks out from under his mattress. Not in the portfolio. Hidden.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She pulls it out.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Her. Sleeping. The shirt riding up. Her face slack, mouth slightly open. Drawn with the same care as the creek at sunset.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Behind it: another. Her reaching for something on a high shelf. Her back arched. The tank top pulling.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Another. Her towel slipping. The bathroom. She didn&#x27;t know he saw that.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_jake_secret_sketches_discovery.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He&#x27;s been watching. Drawing her when she wasn&#x27;t looking. Poses she never held for him. Moments she thought were private.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The shower stops. Footsteps in the hallway. He opens the door. Sees her holding the drawings.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His face goes white.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I— those aren&#x27;t— I was going to throw them away. I wasn&#x27;t—</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He can&#x27;t finish the sentence. His hands are shaking. His hair is dripping on his shirt.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;They&#x27;re beautiful, Jake.&quot;</li><li>&quot;You&#x27;ve been watching me?&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Beautiful</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His mouth opens. Closes. He wasn&#x27;t expecting that.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> You&#x27;re not— angry?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She holds the drawings carefully. The one of her sleeping — the care in the linework, the attention to how light falls on her cheekbone.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> You see me like this? This is how you see me?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His eyes are wet. He nods. The boy who can&#x27;t finish sentences just said everything without a word.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He&#x27;s been watching. But not like Ryan watches — not measuring, not wanting. Memorizing. Like I might disappear and he needs proof I was real.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Watching</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He flinches. The shame hits first — red face, eyes down, hands reaching for the drawings like he can take them back.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Then something else. His jaw sets. He meets her eyes.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I can&#x27;t stop.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Not an apology. A confession. The shy boy has teeth.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I draw you when you&#x27;re not looking because that&#x27;s when you&#x27;re real. When you&#x27;re posing you&#x27;re... performing. When you&#x27;re sleeping you&#x27;re just you.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*The sweet one. The harmless one. He&#x27;s been drawing me in my sleep. And he&#x27;s not sorry.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I should be angry. I&#x27;m not angry. I&#x27;m something else.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_kiss_frank" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Whiskey and This Can't Happen</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_kiss_frank</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Break</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>2 AM. Kitchen. The whiskey is doing what the whiskey does.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His hand is on the table. She puts hers next to it. Their pinkies overlap by a millimeter.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Lily.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>A warning.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> Frank.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He closes his eyes. Opens them. Looks at her mouth.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> This can&#x27;t happen.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> I know.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_kiss_frank_kitchen_kiss.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He kisses her like a man drowning. Controlled, even now — but the control costs everything he has. His hand grips the back of the chair, not her. He&#x27;s holding the furniture because if he holds her he won&#x27;t stop.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He pulls back. Stands up. The chair scrapes the floor.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Good night.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He leaves the kitchen. His coffee is still warm. She drinks it. It tastes like him.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Finish his coffee. Go to bed. Lie awake for hours.</li><li>Leave the coffee. Go to bed. Don&#x27;t think about it.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_kiss_ryan" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Pulled Across the Bench Seat</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_kiss_ryan</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Grab</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He pulls the truck over. Gravel under the tires. Engine off. The radio dies mid-song.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> C&#x27;mere.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>One word. He doesn&#x27;t wait for permission. His hand is behind her neck, pulling her across the bench seat.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_kiss_ryan_truck_kiss.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He tastes like mint gum and adrenaline. His stubble scrapes her chin. She grabs his collar — the truck lurches because his foot slipped off the brake.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He pulls back. The grin. But different — less performance, more hunger.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Took you long enough.</blockquote></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;I didn&#x27;t do anything.&quot; Kiss him again.</li><li>Push him back. &quot;Slow down.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_kiss_jake" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Glasses Bumping Foreheads</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_kiss_jake</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The First</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Late afternoon. The sketchbook is closed. They&#x27;ve been talking — about art school, about the future, about things that don&#x27;t exist yet.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He&#x27;s sitting on the floor. She&#x27;s on the edge of the bed. Their knees are touching and neither of them has moved apart.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I keep drawing you. I can&#x27;t stop drawing you. I&#x27;ve tried.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His voice is steady. The stammer is gone. For once, he knows exactly what he&#x27;s saying.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He leans forward. Careful. His glasses bump her forehead and he pulls back, embarrassed —</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She closes the gap.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_kiss_jake_the_kiss.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His mouth is soft. He tastes like graphite and the coffee he drinks too sweet. His hand finds her jaw — holds it like he&#x27;s positioning a portrait.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The sketchbook slides off the bed. Neither of them picks it up.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Pull him closer.</li><li>Pull back. &quot;We shouldn&#x27;t.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryan_catches" data-game-name="output"> <h2>You're Never Around</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryan_catches</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: He Noticed</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Dinner. @ryan across the table. He&#x27;s been watching her all evening — not the usual watching. Measuring something.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_ryan_catches.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She&#x27;s checking her phone under the table. Looking at the clock. Her mind is somewhere else and her body language says it.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan sets down his fork. Carefully.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> You&#x27;re never around anymore.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Not angry. Hurt. The cocky version of @ryan would make a joke. This one just says it.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I told you something real. Something I haven&#x27;t told anyone. And you haven&#x27;t said a word about it since.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank looks up from his plate. @jake goes still. The table is very quiet.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He&#x27;s right. He told me he&#x27;s leaving and I&#x27;ve been acting like it&#x27;s not happening. Like if I don&#x27;t acknowledge it, it&#x27;s not real.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;I&#x27;ve been busy.&quot;</li><li>&quot;I know. I&#x27;m sorry.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_tease_frank" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Floor</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_tease_frank</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Scrub</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Cleaning day. She&#x27;s on her knees scrubbing the kitchen floor. Short shorts — the ones she picked this morning on purpose.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_tease_frank.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank walks through the hallway. His boots on the hardwood.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She doesn&#x27;t stand up. Keeps scrubbing. Arches her back just enough. The shorts ride up at the back.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His boots stop. Two seconds. Three.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Then they keep going. Faster than usual.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He stopped. I heard him stop. Three seconds of Frank Harmon not being in control of where his eyes go. Three seconds of me owning the hallway.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Keep scrubbing. Smile at the floor.</li><li>Look over her shoulder. Catch his eyes.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_tease_ryan" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Spot</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_tease_ryan</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Bench</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Gym. @ryan on the bench press. She&#x27;s spotting him. Standing above, hands near the bar.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_tease_ryan.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She leans over to adjust his grip. The tank top hangs open. She knows exactly what he can see from that angle. She takes three seconds too long fixing his hand position.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His rep stutters. The bar wobbles. She catches it — her hands over his on the metal.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His eyes are not on the bar.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He challenges everyone. Dares everyone. Today I challenged him. With a tank top and gravity and three seconds he wasn&#x27;t expecting.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Focus.&quot; Step back.</li><li>Stay there. &quot;Need help with the next set too?&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_tease_jake" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Stretch</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_tease_jake</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Break</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Break between poses. @jake is sharpening a pencil. She&#x27;s been sitting still for twenty minutes.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_tease_jake.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She stretches. Arms above her head. Back arches. The shirt rides up — stomach, ribs, the line of her bra underneath. She holds it longer than a stretch needs.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His pencil hovers over the page. He&#x27;s not drawing the stretch. He&#x27;s watching it.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She brings her arms down. Slowly.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*That wasn&#x27;t a stretch. I wanted to see his face when I gave him something he didn&#x27;t ask for. And now I know what his face does.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Sit back down. &quot;Ready when you are.&quot;</li><li>Hold the stretch. Watch his eyes drop.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_flirt_frank" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Silence That Means Something</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_flirt_frank</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Stretch</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Bookkeeping. The office door is closed. Amber lamplight. Numbers that stopped mattering five minutes ago.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_flirt_frank.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She puts down the pen. Looks at him. Doesn&#x27;t say anything.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank is writing in the ledger. He feels her looking. His pen slows. Stops.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He doesn&#x27;t look up. His jaw tightens.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Something wrong with the numbers?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His voice is level. Controlled. But the pen hasn&#x27;t moved.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I&#x27;m not looking at the numbers, Frank. You know I&#x27;m not looking at the numbers.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Nothing&#x27;s wrong. I just like watching you work.&quot;</li><li>Pick up the pen. Get back to work. Let him wonder.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_flirt_ryan" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Matching Energy</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_flirt_ryan</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Dare Back</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Truck. Windows down. He&#x27;s been making comments all day — nothing new. The usual Ryan testing.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_flirt_ryan.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> You know, for a college girl, you&#x27;re not bad company.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She turns to face him. Plants her elbow on the bench seat. Chin on her hand.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> For a guy who can&#x27;t read fast enough for community college, you&#x27;re not bad either.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His grin freezes. Then cracks wider. Something shifts in his eyes — surprise. She KNOWS about the reading. She filed it away. And she used it. Not cruel. Playful.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Oh, so that&#x27;s how it is?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*That&#x27;s how it is.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Scared?&quot;</li><li>Wink. Turn back to the window.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_flirt_jake" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Pencil Pause</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_flirt_jake</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Deliberate</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He&#x27;s drawing her profile. She&#x27;s been holding still for ten minutes. His pencil moves in long, careful strokes.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_flirt_jake.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She shifts. Turns her head toward him. Slowly. Not the pose he asked for.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His pencil stops.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> That&#x27;s— that&#x27;s not the angle I—</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She&#x27;s looking directly at him now. Not at the drawing. At him.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His ears go red. The pencil trembles between his fingers.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I did that on purpose. I wanted to see what his face does when I look at him instead of away.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;You&#x27;re cute when you&#x27;re flustered.&quot;</li><li>Hold the look. Say nothing. Let it build.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_jakes_art" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Portfolio</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_jakes_art</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Real Work</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@jake pulls a portfolio case from under his bed. Black leather, zipped. His hands are shaking — not the drawing kind of shaking. The showing-someone kind.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I&#x27;ve never— nobody&#x27;s seen these. Not Dad. Not @ryan. My professor saw three.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He unzips it. Charcoal on heavyweight paper. The creek at four different times of day. The workshop in morning fog. @frank&#x27;s hands on a coffee mug — just the hands, just the mug, and everything about the man is in the grip.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_jakes_confession_the_sketchbook.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>They&#x27;re good. Not student-good. Actually good.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He&#x27;s watching her face. Cataloging her reaction like data. Every micro-expression is a verdict.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He sees things. The workshop in fog, Frank&#x27;s hands on a mug. He sees what I&#x27;m starting to see — this house is beautiful and terrible and he&#x27;s been drawing it his whole life.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Point to the hands drawing. &quot;This one. Tell me about this one.&quot;</li><li>&quot;Jake. These are incredible.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_creek_discovery" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Swimming Hole</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_creek_discovery</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Water</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The creek. Fifteen minutes through the trees. She found it on a walk — the property slopes down, the woods thicken, and then: water over smooth stones, dappled light, the sound of nothing man-made.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Shallow enough to wade. Deep at the bend — deep enough to swim if you&#x27;re willing.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_creek_swim_the_heat.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The water is cold. Her toes go numb in seconds. But the sun is on her shoulders and nobody is watching and for the first time in two weeks, the house isn&#x27;t pressing in.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*This is mine. The one place in this property that doesn&#x27;t belong to any of them.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Wade in deeper.</li><li>Sit on the bank. Just breathe.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_bookkeeping_offer" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Office Door</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_bookkeeping_offer</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Numbers</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She passes his office during cleaning. The door is open — unusual. He&#x27;s at the desk, papers fanned out, pinching the bridge of his nose.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Lily. You good with numbers?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He gestures at the invoices. Client payments, supply costs, payroll for the crew. The filing cabinet behind him is overflowing.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Twenty-five dollars per session. Three times a week. You handle the receivables, I handle the rest.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The office smells like leather and old paper. The desk lamp casts everything in amber. There are two chairs — his behind the desk, and one in front.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_bookkeeping_offer_the_offer.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Twenty-five dollars. Three times a week. That&#x27;s seventy-five a week. That changes everything.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Behind a door that locks.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;When do we start?&quot;</li><li>&quot;I&#x27;d like that. Thank you, Frank.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryan_departure" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Engine Running</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryan_departure</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Dawn</h4> <div class="review-block block-other"></div> <div class="review-block block-other"></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Go outside. Now.</li><li>Watch from the window. Let him go.</li><li>Close the curtain.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Stay</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She runs. Bare feet on cold ground. The gravel bites but she doesn&#x27;t stop.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She opens the passenger door. Gets in. He looks at her. The engine is running. The bag is packed. His hand is on the gearshift.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She puts her hand on his. Moves it off the gearshift. To the bench seat between them. Then lower.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> Stay.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Her hand moves. He closes his eyes. The performance is gone — the grin, the cocky set of his jaw, the testing. Just a man in a truck with his eyes closed and his breath catching.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>player:</strong> We&#x27;ll figure it out. You and me. Your own jobs. I&#x27;ll do the numbers, find the clients. You&#x27;re better than your dad&#x27;s leftovers.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He makes a sound. Not the Ryan sound. The real one underneath — the one from the lookout point, from the scar story, from every moment the act drops and there&#x27;s just a boy who wanted to be more than his father&#x27;s shadow.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His hand finds hers. Grips it. The engine is still running.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He turns it off.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The driveway is quiet. The truck stays.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;You and me.&quot;</li><li>&quot;Take me with you.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryan_ultimatum" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Come With Me</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryan_ultimatum</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Question</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Truck. The lookout point again. He drove here on purpose. Engine off. Radio off.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_ryan_ultimatum.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan doesn&#x27;t look at the valley this time. He looks at her.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He&#x27;s not performing. No grin. No testing. The version of Ryan that exists underneath all of it.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I leave in ten days.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>A breath.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Come with me.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Not a dare. Not a negotiation. Not leverage. A man sitting in his truck asking someone to choose him over everything else.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The valley below is getting dark. The farmland lights are coming on one by one.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He&#x27;s not asking me to run away. He&#x27;s asking me to run toward something. With him.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;I&#x27;ll think about it.&quot;</li><li>&quot;I can&#x27;t leave.&quot;</li><li>&quot;Ask me again in a week.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryan_truth" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Twenty-Three and Stuck</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryan_truth</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Whole Story</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Parked. Somewhere she&#x27;s never been — a lookout point off the back highway. The valley below. Farmland and distant lights.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_ryan_truth.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan turns the engine off. Sits. Both hands on the wheel like he&#x27;s still driving.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I&#x27;m twenty-three.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He says it like a diagnosis.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Twenty-three. Dad&#x27;s house. Dad&#x27;s truck. Dad&#x27;s jobs. Dad&#x27;s roads. I know every back road in this county because I&#x27;ve been driving them since I was sixteen and nothing&#x27;s changed.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He pulls up his sleeve. The scar on his forearm — faded, but the raised line catches the light.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Eighteen. Job site. Board slipped off the saw table and I caught it with my arm instead of letting it fall. Know why? Because Dad&#x27;s clients were watching. Because if I let it fall, it&#x27;d cost us a day. I was eighteen and I took a board to the arm so Frank Harmon&#x27;s deadline wouldn&#x27;t slip.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His voice is steady. Not angry. Just tired.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> That&#x27;s the day I knew. If I don&#x27;t leave, I&#x27;m him. Forty-five, this house, these roads, until the house falls down.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He looks at her.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> The job is real. My own crew. Real money. I leave before your mom gets back. Nobody needs to know until I&#x27;m gone.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He&#x27;s been carrying this the whole time. The detours, the phone calls, the map. All of it was him building an exit while I was falling into the house.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Why didn&#x27;t you tell me?&quot;</li><li>&quot;When?&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryan_map" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Under the Seat</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryan_map</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Folder</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan is inside the gas station. She&#x27;s in the truck. Waiting.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_ryan_map.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Her phone slips between the seats. She reaches under the bench to grab it. Her fingers find something else.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>A folder. Manila, bent at the corners. Inside: a road map with a city circled in red marker. Highway routes highlighted. A printed email — job offer letterhead, a company she doesn&#x27;t recognize.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She reads it. Construction foreman. His own crew. Start date six weeks from now. Salary that&#x27;s three times what Frank pays.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Under the email: a handwritten list. Things to pack. Things to sell. &#x27;Tell Dad&#x27; at the bottom, circled twice, with a question mark.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He&#x27;s leaving. Not someday. Soon. And &#x27;Tell Dad&#x27; has a question mark because he&#x27;s not sure he will.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The gas station door opens. He&#x27;s walking back with two coffees. He sees her face. Then the folder in her hands.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The coffees stop. His grin dies.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> That wasn&#x27;t— you shouldn&#x27;t have seen that.</blockquote></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;You&#x27;re leaving.&quot;</li><li>Put the folder back. Take the coffee. Say nothing.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryan_phone" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Two Weeks</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryan_phone</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Fragments</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Truck. Back roads. His phone buzzes on the bench seat between them. He glances at the screen. His jaw tightens.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_ryan_phone.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I gotta take this.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He pulls over. Gravel shoulder. Gets out. Walks ten feet from the truck but doesn&#x27;t close his door all the way.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She can hear fragments through the gap. His voice low, careful — not the Ryan voice. A different one.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Yeah... two weeks... I know... I said I&#x27;d be there.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>A pause. He kicks gravel. Runs his hand through his hair.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I&#x27;ll figure it out. Just hold the spot.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He comes back. Phone in his pocket. Starts the truck. Doesn&#x27;t look at her.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Two weeks. Hold the spot. What spot?*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Who was that?&quot;</li><li>Let it go. He&#x27;ll tell her when he&#x27;s ready.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryan_detour" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Wrong Turn</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryan_detour</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Wrong Road</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Highway. @ryan driving. She&#x27;s looking at her phone when the truck slows and turns onto a gravel road she doesn&#x27;t recognize.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_ryan_detour.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Not the campus road. Not the creek road. Somewhere she hasn&#x27;t been.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>A construction site. Half-built commercial building, heavy equipment, a crew she doesn&#x27;t recognize. @ryan pulls in. Cuts the engine.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> One minute.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He gets out. Walks to a man in a hard hat. They talk — shoulders square, handshake, something exchanged. A business card. A folder.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He comes back. Starts the truck. Doesn&#x27;t explain.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Wrong turn.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>It wasn&#x27;t a wrong turn. She&#x27;s been in this truck enough times to know every road he takes. This one was new. And deliberate.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He talked to that man like they&#x27;d met before. Like there was a plan.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;You missed the turn.&quot;</li><li>Say nothing. Watch the road.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_ryans_push" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Hand on the Knee</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_ryans_push</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Drive Back</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Driving back from town. The road narrows to two lanes. Pine trees crowd both sides.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan has one hand on the wheel. The radio is off. That&#x27;s unusual — @ryan always has the radio on.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His other hand is on the bench seat between them. Close to her knee. Not touching. But close.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_ryans_push_the_ride.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He glances at her. Then his hand moves. Settles on her knee. Not gripping. Just... there. Testing.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Relax.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>One word. The grin is there but it&#x27;s different — less cocky, more careful. Like he&#x27;s calculating what happens next based on what she does in the next three seconds.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*His hand is on my knee. It&#x27;s warm. He&#x27;s watching my face, not the road.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Slap his hand away.</li><li>Let his hand stay.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Slap</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She smacks his hand off her knee. Hard enough that it stings.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan pulls back. Both hands on the wheel. The grin flickers — surprise, then something that might be respect.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Alright. Message received.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He turns the radio on. Loud. Doesn&#x27;t say another word until they pull into the driveway.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>But she notices: he holds the door for her when she gets out. He&#x27;s never done that before.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He&#x27;s not angry. He&#x27;s... recalculating.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Stay</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She doesn&#x27;t move. His hand stays on her knee. His thumb traces a small circle on her kneecap.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The grin sharpens. He knows what her not-moving means.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> That&#x27;s what I thought.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He drives slower for the last mile. His hand doesn&#x27;t move. Neither does she.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I should have moved his hand. I didn&#x27;t want to move his hand.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_awareness_frank" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Hallway at Midnight</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_awareness_frank</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Sleep Shirt</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Midnight. She can&#x27;t sleep. The hallway is dark except for the kitchen light — a warm line under the door.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She&#x27;s wearing a sleep shirt. Just a sleep shirt. The hallway is cold. She doesn&#x27;t go back for pants.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank is at the table. Whiskey. Undershirt. Barefoot. The controlled version of himself coming loose at the seams.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He looks up when she enters. His eyes drop to the hem of the sleep shirt — mid-thigh — then snap back to her face.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_awareness_frank_the_encounter.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Can&#x27;t sleep?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Not a question. A statement. His voice is different at midnight — lower, less controlled.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He looked. Just for a second. He looked.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I felt it.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Sit across from him. &quot;Pour me one.&quot;</li><li>Get the water. Go back to bed.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_awareness_ryan" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Creek</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_awareness_ryan</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Shirt Off</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The creek. @ryan drove them here — back roads, no music for once. He strips his shirt without ceremony and wades in.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Sweat and creek water. His back is tanned from the job site. Muscles that come from labor, not vanity.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_awareness_ryan_the_creek.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> You coming in or just gonna watch?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The grin. He knows she&#x27;s looking. He wants her to know he knows.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*When did I start looking? When did I stop pretending I wasn&#x27;t?*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Wade in. Match his energy.</li><li>Sit on the bank. Watch from a safe distance.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_awareness_jake" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Collarbone</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_awareness_jake</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Hold Still</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Afternoon light through @jake&#x27;s window. He&#x27;s drawing — her hands this time. She&#x27;s sitting on the edge of his bed, holding still.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Graphite and clean laundry. The scratch of his pencil.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Your collar is casting a shadow. Can you—</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He gestures at her neckline. She pulls the collar to one side. Her collarbone exposed.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His pencil stops.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_awareness_jake_the_moment.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Three seconds. He&#x27;s looking at her collarbone, her neck, the shadow line. Not as an artist. As a boy who just realized what he&#x27;s been feeling isn&#x27;t just artistic admiration.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His pencil starts again. Faster. He doesn&#x27;t ask her to fix the collar.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He&#x27;s looking at me differently. Not like @ryan looks — not measuring. Like he&#x27;s seeing something he wants to draw a hundred times.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I don&#x27;t hate it.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Hold the pose. Let him look.</li><li>Fix the collar. &quot;Better?&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_jake_notices" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Pencil Stops</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_jake_notices</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Breakfast</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Morning. She comes downstairs. Hair done — not elaborate, just cared for. The yoga shows in her posture. The gym in her shoulders. The grooming in the way light catches her skin.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_jake_notices.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@jake is at the table. Sketchbook propped against the salt shaker. Pencil moving. Earbuds in. The usual.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He looks up.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The pencil stops.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He&#x27;s never looked at her like that. Two weeks of earbuds and closed doors and &#x27;knock&#x27; — and now his hand is still and his eyes are tracking the line of her jaw. The curve of her collarbone. The way the morning light hits the side of her neck.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He looks back down. Fast. The next line he draws curves wrong — her jawline, not whatever he was sketching. He tears the page out. Crumples it. Shoves it in his pocket.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*He drew my jaw. Without meaning to. I saw it before he crumpled it.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Two weeks of nothing. And now his pencil can&#x27;t draw a straight line when I walk in.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Eat breakfast. Pretend she didn&#x27;t notice.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Invitation</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Afternoon. He&#x27;s walking past her in the hallway. Sketchbook under his arm. He stops.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He doesn&#x27;t look at her. Looks at the floor. At the wall. At his sketchbook.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I could draw you. This afternoon. If you&#x27;re not busy.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He says it to the floor. Already walking away before she answers. His ears are red — the first color she&#x27;s seen on him since the bathroom.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Three weeks of nothing. Earbuds and closed doors and one-word answers. And now this.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*&#x27;If you&#x27;re not busy.&#x27; Like he&#x27;s been rehearsing it. Like he&#x27;s been wanting to ask since the crumpled page at breakfast and couldn&#x27;t find the words until now.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;I&#x27;d like that.&quot;</li><li>&quot;What changed your mind?&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_week" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Routines</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_week</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Patterns</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>One week. She knows things now.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_week.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@frank makes coffee at five. The grinder wakes her through the floor. He&#x27;s in the workshop by six, back for dinner at six sharp. His routine is the house&#x27;s clock.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@jake hums when he draws. Something with no melody. She hears it through the wall at midnight — pencil scratching, humming, the click of his desk lamp going off.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan is unpredictable. Some mornings he&#x27;s shirtless in the kitchen. Some mornings his truck is already gone. He comes home dusty, loud, fills whatever room he&#x27;s in.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She&#x27;s learning the house. Which floorboards creak. Which doors stick. That the bathroom lock doesn&#x27;t catch and the walls are thin and the kitchen is never really empty.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I&#x27;m learning them. I wonder what they&#x27;re learning about me.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_the_math" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Thirteen Dollars</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_the_math</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Numbers</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She sits on the bed with her phone calculator and the back of a receipt.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Bus pass: $80 a month. Art supplies: $60. Phone bill: $45. Food contribution — @frank mentioned it at dinner, casual, like it was obvious — $50 a week.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>That&#x27;s $385 a month. She has $400.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Thirteen dollars. For ten weeks.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Mom&#x27;s $200 transfer — when it comes — covers half a month. Maybe. If the exchange rate cooperates. If Diana remembers.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I&#x27;m not calling her. I&#x27;m not asking @frank. I&#x27;m not asking anyone.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_the_math_morning_realization.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>Go find Frank. Ask about work.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Frank&#x27;s Offer</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She finds him in the kitchen. He&#x27;s reading the paper. Coffee black, half finished.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> The house could use regular cleaning. Twenty dollars per session. I&#x27;ll show you what needs doing.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He says it like he&#x27;s been waiting for her to ask. Maybe he has.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> And the food money — fifty a week. That&#x27;s non-negotiable. Everyone pulls their weight.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>His tone isn&#x27;t unkind. It&#x27;s practical. The same tone he uses for everything — delivering information, not opinions.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Deal. When do I start?&quot;</li><li>&quot;That&#x27;s generous. Thank you, Frank.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_moms_call" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Transfer's Delayed</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_moms_call</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: Mom&#x27;s Voice</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Her phone buzzes. Mom.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_moms_call.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Diana&#x27;s voice is tired. The connection crackles — Dubai to rural Virginia. She called at 3 AM her time.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Honey, the transfer&#x27;s going to be a few days late. The exchange rate is complicated. I&#x27;m sorry.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I wouldn&#x27;t have asked you to stay if there was any other option. You know that, right?</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*I know, Mom. I know.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She stares at the phone after hanging up. The bank app shows $398. The list of expenses she wrote on the back of a receipt is getting longer.</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;It&#x27;s fine, Mom. I&#x27;m fine.&quot;</li><li>Tell her the truth about the money situation.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_bus_problem" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Bus or the Truck</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_bus_problem</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Offer</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She needs to get to campus. The bus stop is a fifteen-minute walk down the road. Forty minutes each way, two dollars per ride.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@ryan is in the hallway. Keys in hand. Truck keys — she can hear the engine ticking from the driveway.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> I can drive you. Fifteen minutes. Free.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>He says it casually. Like it&#x27;s nothing.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Free? Or does &#x27;free&#x27; mean he decides when I leave and when I come back?*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_bus_problem_the_ride.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;I&#x27;ll take the bus. Thanks though.&quot;</li><li>&quot;Sure. Let me grab my bag.&quot;</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_first_morning" data-game-name="output"> <h2>The Bathroom Door</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_first_morning</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: The Door That Doesn&#x27;t Lock</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Morning. The bathroom door is shut but not locked — the hook latch doesn&#x27;t catch. She&#x27;d forgotten.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She pushes it open.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>@jake at the sink. T-shirt, boxers. Toothbrush in his mouth. He looks at her in the mirror.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Not embarrassed. Not surprised. Annoyed.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_first_morning_bathroom_problem.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-dialog"><blockquote><strong>npc:</strong> Knock.</blockquote></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>One word. He doesn&#x27;t look away from the mirror. Doesn&#x27;t stammer. Doesn&#x27;t blush. Goes back to brushing his teeth like she&#x27;s already gone.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Not &#x27;sorry.&#x27; Not &#x27;the lock doesn&#x27;t work.&#x27; Just &#x27;knock.&#x27; Like I&#x27;m intruding on his house. His bathroom. His morning.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*The walls are thin. The lock doesn&#x27;t work. One bathroom for four people. And one of them doesn&#x27;t want me here.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: choices</em><ul><li>&quot;Sorry.&quot; Back out. Close the door.</li><li>Say nothing. Close the door.</li></ul></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div><div id="canvas-review-detail" data-canvas-id="story_unpacking" data-game-name="output"> <h2>Unpacking</h2> <p class="canvas-id-display">Canvas ID: <code>story_unpacking</code></p> <div class="review-node"> <h4>NODE: One Suitcase</h4> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>The room is small. Single bed, desk for homework, closet that won&#x27;t fit much. The window faces the backyard — she can see the creek from here.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-image"><img src="./videos/story/story_unpacking.jpg" style="max-width:100%;max-height:70vh;object-fit:contain;height:auto;border-radius:8px;" /></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>She sets the suitcase on the bed. Unzips it. Everything she owns for the next two months.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Hoodie. Jeans. The good sundress — why did she pack that? Three t-shirts. Shorts. Sleep clothes. Underwear she shoves into the drawer without looking.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Through the wall: the scratch of pencil on paper. @jake&#x27;s room. She can hear him drawing.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Down the hall: the TV, low volume. @ryan watching something.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>Downstairs: dishes. @frank cleaning up alone.</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*The mattress smells like someone else&#x27;s laundry detergent. The pillow is too soft. The walls are too thin.*</p></div> <div class="review-block review-block-paragraph"><p>*Home for fifty-six days.*</p></div> <div class="exit-info"><em>Exit: location</em></div> </div> <div class="review-back-btn"> <<button "← Back to List">><<goto "CanvasReviewList">><</button>> </div> </div>
